posts.content:
We’re Hiring: Capital Markets Professionals (Urgent Requirement)

At #FinleafLegal, we are a new-age legal and compliance advisory firm working closely with investment funds, asset managers, and financial institutions across India’s evolving capital markets landscape. 

Our focus lies in providing practical, business-oriented regulatory support—helping our clients navigate complexity with clarity and confidence.

As we continue to grow, we are actively hiring Capital Markets Professionals to support our expanding portfolio of clients across all types of funds, including AIFs, PMS, and other investment vehicles.

🔍 Role Overview
You will be involved in handling regulatory compliance, fund documentation, and ongoing advisory for clients operating in the capital markets space. This is a hands-on role offering exposure to diverse fund structures and regulatory frameworks.

Key Requirements
*Mandatory: Valid NISM Certification
*Prior experience in capital markets / fund compliance / regulatory advisory
*Familiarity with SEBI regulations, especially AIF/PMS frameworks
*Strong attention to detail and documentation skills

⚡ What We’re Looking For
Professionals who can join immediately or at short notice
Individuals who are proactive, detail-oriented, and comfortable in a fast-paced environment. 

📩 How to Apply
If you’re interested—or know someone who might be a good fit please share your CV at finleaftheconsultants@gmail.com.

We’re looking to onboard quickly, so early applications are highly encouraged.

posts.content:
𝗠𝘆𝘁𝗵𝗼𝘀 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗕𝗮𝗻𝗸𝗲𝗿: 𝗔 𝗣𝗿𝗮𝗰𝘁𝗶𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝗲𝗿'𝘀 𝗩𝗶𝗲𝘄 𝗼𝗻 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗔𝗜 𝗧𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝗪𝗼𝗿𝗿𝗶𝗲𝗱 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗜𝗠𝗙

𝗗𝗮𝘆 𝟰 𝗼𝗳 𝟳 | 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝘁𝗵𝗿𝗲𝗲 𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗲𝘀 𝗼𝗳 𝗱𝗲𝗳𝗲𝗻𝗰𝗲 𝘄𝗲𝗿𝗲 𝗯𝘂𝗶𝗹𝘁 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗵𝘂𝗺𝗮𝗻-𝘀𝗽𝗲𝗲𝗱 𝘁𝗵𝗿𝗲𝗮𝘁𝘀.

The three lines of defence model is one of the most widely adopted frameworks in banking. Business owns the risk. Risk and compliance oversee it. Internal audit independently assures it.

It's a good model. It has served the industry well for two decades.

It was also designed in an era when threats moved at human speed.

Think about the #cadence each line actually operates at.

The first line runs daily controls, monthly reconciliations, quarterly attestations. The second line runs periodic reviews, thematic deep-dives, annual risk assessments. The third line runs an audit plan that typically covers each major area once every one to three years.

This rhythm made sense when adversaries were also operating in months and years. A threat took time to develop. A vulnerability took time to weaponise. The defence cadence and the attack cadence were roughly matched.

𝙈𝙮𝙩𝙝𝙤𝙨 𝙗𝙧𝙚𝙖𝙠𝙨 𝙩𝙝𝙖𝙩 𝙨𝙮𝙢𝙢𝙚𝙩𝙧𝙮.

When #reconnaissance happens continuously and at machine speed, a defence model that tests itself annually is operating on the wrong clock. By the time a third-line audit finds a gap, the gap may have been known externally for months.

This isn't a criticism of audit or second line functions. They are doing exactly what the framework asks of them, at the cadence the framework prescribes. The framework itself is the question.

A few uncomfortable observations from inside the system.

Second-line monitoring is still heavily dependent on sampling and periodic review. In an environment where threats are continuous, sampling has structural blind spots. The things you didn't sample are precisely the things an intelligent adversary will find.

Third-line audit cycles of two to three years were designed when the business model changed slowly. The business model is no longer changing slowly. Neither is the threat model.

First-line controls often rely on detective measures reported after the fact. Detection-after-the-fact is fine when the cost of the event is contained. It is less fine when the event is a cyber intrusion that spreads in minutes.

The question isn't whether three lines of defence should exist. It should. The question is whether the cadence, tooling, and intensity of each line is still calibrated to the threat environment we actually operate in.

My own view. 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗺𝗼𝗱𝗲𝗹 𝗻𝗲𝗲𝗱𝘀 𝗮 𝗳𝗼𝘂𝗿𝘁𝗵 𝗰𝗮𝗽𝗮𝗯𝗶𝗹𝗶𝘁𝘆 𝘄𝗼𝘃𝗲𝗻 𝘁𝗵𝗿𝗼𝘂𝗴𝗵 𝗮𝗹𝗹 𝘁𝗵𝗿𝗲𝗲 𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗲𝘀. 𝗖𝗼𝗻𝘁𝗶𝗻𝘂𝗼𝘂𝘀 𝗮𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗿𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲. Real-time, automated, always-on. Not replacing human judgement. Feeding it faster.

Tomorrow. Third-party attestations and the lag that everyone ignores.

#SaurabhSays #Mythos #ThreeLinesOfDefence #InternalAudit #RiskGovernance

posts.content:
India is emerging as a frontrunner in digital sovereignty investments, but a global gap between intent and execution persists, K G Narendranath reports for Financial Express, citing a study by SUSE.

About 62% of Indian enterprises are actively investing in digital sovereignty, higher than Germany and Japan (57%), the U.S. (52%), and France (39%), finds the study, which surveyed 309 IT leaders across key markets.

However, although 98% of organisations prioritise sovereignty, only 52% have implemented strategies. This highlights a “growing gap” between ambition and action, the report adds. Notably, 41% of organisations act only when pushed by regulation or customer demand, highlighting the role of external triggers.

“Organisations are often forced to choose between accelerating AI and maintaining digital sovereignty, but it’s a false tradeoff,” says Margaret Dawson (戴珍珠) Officer, SUSE, adding that “sovereign AI makes it possible to achieve both”.

But, the study also points to a forward-looking shift.

Nearly 64% of IT leaders are identifying AI transparency — control over model training and provenance — as a key driver of digital resilience over the next five years. Even so, despite a reported 20% increase in budgets, many organisations continue to prioritise AI adoption over sovereignty, suggesting that the pace of innovation may outstrip preparedness to manage associated risks, the report adds.

Meanwhile, IITM Pravartak Technologies Foundation and Sarvam AI recently partnered to accelerate India’s push for sovereign AI, focusing on building production-ready systems rooted in local data, languages and infrastructure, reports YourStory. The collaboration aims to deploy end-to-end AI applications across sectors such as government, defence, finance and healthcare, reducing reliance on global platforms.

In fact, Sarvam AI is also in early talks to raise $250–300 million, suggests another report by Outlook Business. This will potentially value the firm at $1.2 billion, a 10x jump that indicates the surging momentum in India’s sovereign AI ecosystem.

How do you think India’s sovereign AI ecosystem will shape up? Share your thoughts in the comments section.

✍: Nakul Ghai
📷: Getty Images 

Source: Financial Express (India): https://lnkd.in/dBsfF9_M
Outlook Business: https://lnkd.in/dZPPfkpY
YourStory Media: https://lnkd.in/giQGZGQi

#SovereignAI #AI #Technology  | 14 comments on LinkedIn

posts.content:
We are proud to recognize Asim Siddiqui a highly accomplished finance professional with over 15 years of comprehensive experience in accounting, taxation, and corporate financial management. A specialist in navigating complex regulatory environments, he has established a distinguished career characterized by his strategic oversight of IPO processes, statutory compliance, and the streamlining of large-scale financial operations.

Currently, Asim Siddiqui serves as Finance Manager at Chemkart India Limited in Mumbai. In this pivotal role, he was a core member of the company’s IPO team, playing a critical role in the filing of the Draft Red Herring Prospectus (DRHP), the finalization of Restated Financial Statements (RFS), and the management of IPO proceeds. His expertise extends to the end-to-end financial management of wholly owned subsidiaries and the preparation of consolidated financial statements, ensuring total alignment with global accounting standards.

Previously, Asim held senior leadership positions, including Manager of Internal F&A at Hinduja Global Solutions (HGS) and Deputy Manager of Accounts & Taxation at FOSS India. During his six-year tenure at FOSS India, he led statutory audits, managed multi-state tax filings (GST, TDS, and Income Tax), and oversaw complex import operations and FEMA compliance. 

Earlier in his career, he contributed his technical proficiency to organizations such as Sunjewels Private Limited, Mettler-Toledo International, Inc, and Sanghvi Electronics, where he spearheaded SAP FI integrations and automated financial reporting modules.

Asim Siddiqui holds an MBA in Finance from the INDIAN SCHOOL OF BUSINESS MANAGEMENT AND ADMINISTRATION and a Master of Commerce in Accounting and Finance from the University of Mumbai. His deep technical mastery of SAP, MS Navision, and Tally, combined with his foundation in audit at N. G. Rao & Associates, has made him a leading figure in India's financial sector, known for driving accuracy and scalability in the most demanding corporate environments.

👏 We celebrate Asim Siddiqui for his exceptional technical leadership, his pivotal contributions to corporate capital market journeys, and his unwavering dedication to financial excellence and integrity.

#IndiaGlobalLeaders #GlobalLeadersInc #AsimSiddiqui #FinanceManager #IPOExpertise #CorporateFinance #Taxation #FinancialReporting #SAPFI #Compliance #MumbaiBusiness #Leadership #India India Global Leaders Global Leaders Inc.

posts.author_name:
🚀 Amazing Conversation...with Nitin Sawant around current momentum of Account Aggregator (AA) 

While most conversations focus on UPI, a powerful transformation is quietly reshaping India’s… | Prasanna Lohar
posts.content:
🚀 Amazing Conversation...with Nitin Sawant around current momentum of Account Aggregator (AA) 

While most conversations focus on UPI, a powerful transformation is quietly reshaping India’s financial ecosystem — Account Aggregator (AA). As of early 2026, India's Account Aggregator (AA) framework has matured into a cornerstone of digital financial infrastructure, with over 17 licensed AAs and 135+ Financial Information Providers (FIPs) enabling secure, consent-based data sharing

Today, AA is becoming core financial infrastructure 
➟180M+ accounts linked
➟ ₹1.4+ lakh crore lending enabled
➟Powering ~10% of retail & MSME credit
➟Largest open finance implementation globally
➟A reference model for emerging markets
➟Part of G20 Digital Public Infrastructure narrative

 🚀 What makes AA game-changing?
➟ Consent-driven data sharing - For the first time, individuals and businesses control their financial data — securely and digitally
➟Faster, smarter lending From days to minutes — cashflow-based underwriting is unlocking credit for MSMEs and new-to-credit customers.
➟Foundation for Open Finance - AA is doing for data what UPI did for payments — creating a scalable, interoperable ecosystem

🎯 Key Challenges (Reality Check) Despite momentum, gaps remain
1. Adoption Gaps
➟  PSU banks & smaller NBFCs not fully integrated
➟  Low consumer awareness

2. Data Challenges
➟ Inconsistent data quality
➟ API standardization issues

3. Use-case Concentration
➟ Heavy dependency on lending
➟ Wealth, insurance still underutilized

🏆 Lending is the Killer Use Case (So Far)  
1. Instant underwriting using 
➟ Bank statements
➟ Cashflows
➟  GST data , Reduces: Fraud , Manual docs and TAT

2. MSME Credit Revolution
➟  Cashflow-based lending
➟  Thin-file borrower inclusion
 
Rise of OMS Fintech Account Aggregator Pvt. Ltd.  

1. Documentation : Physical / PDF Uploads ➟  100% Paperless / Digital
2. Data Verification: Manual (Hours/Days) ➟  Instant (Seconds)
3. Data Security : Low (Email/WhatsApp) ➟  High (End-to-End Encrypted)
4. Underwriting : Asset-Based ➟  Real-Time Cash-Flow Based
5. Approval TAT : 3 to 15 Days ➟  5 Minutes to 2 Hours
6. Fraud Risk : High (Forged Documents) ➟  Zero (Source-Verified Data)
7. User Effort : Very High ➟  Minimal (Single-Tap Consent)

📌 Key Developments in 2026 
➟ Comprehensive Data Access: Beyond bank statements, AA now facilitates the sharing of over 20 types of financial information, including GST data, insurance policies, mutual funds, and pension funds
➟ Cross-Sectoral Integration: SEBI-regulated entities (capital markets) are now fully integrated, allowing for a comprehensive view of a user's financial profile

Let's look at Enhanced Security & Consent The framework operates on a "privacy by design" principle, with new Self-Regulatory Organization (SRO) guidelines focusing on vernacular consent and enhanced security

India Finnovation Network | India FinTech Forum | Sahamati | Finarkein

posts.author_name:
Today, we launched the Unicorn Growth Fund.
INR 6,000cr. Backed by KRAFTON Inc. and NAVER Corp — two of South Korea's largest technology companies, and managed and advised by Mirae Asset Venture… | Puneet Kumar | 47 comments
posts.content:
Today, we launched the Unicorn Growth Fund.
INR 6,000cr. Backed by KRAFTON Inc. and NAVER Corp — two of South Korea's largest technology companies, and managed and advised by Mirae Asset Venture Investments. It is one of the largest India-focused technology fund pools ever raised from Asia.
The announcement was made in New Delhi yesterday, on the sidelines of the Korean President's state visit to India — alongside Hon'ble Minister Piyush Goyal, Hon'ble Minister Jung-Kwan Kim, and the global CEOs of KRAFTON and Naver. 
The Fund will back growth-stage Indian founders across four themes:
→ Technology Platforms — consumer internet, marketplaces, next-gen platform infra 
→ Consumer Discretionary — digitally-native brands for India's rising middle class 
→ AI & Software — generative AI, applied AI, enterprise SaaS, developer tooling → Deep Tech — semiconductors, space-tech, robotics, advanced materials

India is at an inflection point. Over the next decade, a new generation of Indian technology champions will be built in India — for India and the world. Those founders will need capital. They will also need product, AI, gaming, and platform expertise from partners who have built at scale globally. That is what our on-the-ground Mirae Asset team in India will activate with expertise from KRAFTON and Naver. 
To the founders, if you are building a category-leading company with global ambition, we would love to hear from you.
Grateful to Changhan Kim, Soo Yeon Choi, Sean (Hyunil) Sohn, the KRAFTON and Naver teams, Hon'ble Minister Piyush Goyal, Hon'ble Minister Jung-Kwan Kim, the Mirae Asset leadership (Swarup Mohanty, Ryan Kim, Ph.D, Taewan Kwon, and Mirae Asset Venture Investments (India) colleagues who made this happen.

Now the real work begins. | 47 comments on LinkedIn
posts.summary: Today, we launched the Unicorn Growth Fund. INR 6,000cr. Backed by KRAFTON Inc.

EquityPandit is now in the financial capital of India — Mumbai.

20 years of believing every Indian investor deserves real research — not tips, not rumours.

Today that belief gets a new address.

Grateful to the team that made this happen and every client who trusted us.

We're hiring — 30 positions open at our Mumbai office.

If you're looking to build a career in financial services — DM me or send your CV to [hr@equitypandit.com]

posts.author_name:
I’ve been talking about my journey to a lot of my peers and team members lately.
And every time I do, I’m reminded — it’s been anything but a straight line.

Citibank taught me discipline and the art… | Jatin Chhabria
posts.content:
I’ve been talking about my journey to a lot of my peers and team members lately.
And every time I do, I’m reminded — it’s been anything but a straight line.

Citibank taught me discipline and the art of building trust with clients.

Capillary Technologies took me across borders — literally. Malaysia, new markets, new problems to solve.

Razorpay was where I truly grew up professionally. Five years, multiple roles, and a front-row seat to one of India’s most exciting fintech stories.

Then a career break — not a pause, but a pivot. Fatherhood. Family. Perspective.

And today, owning P&L for three products at 
Cashfree Payments — driving strategy, building teams, and pushing boundaries in digital identity and payouts.
13 years. Multiple industries. A few storms. A lot of lessons.

Here’s what the roller coaster taught me:

→ Every role was preparing me for the next one — even when I couldn’t see it

→ The hardest moments built the most durable skills

→ Slowing down sometimes is what lets you speed up later

→ The people you build with matter more than the company name on your badge

Nobody gives you a manual. You just show up and figure it out.
Still figuring it out — just with better WiFi now 😄

To everyone on their own chaotic, confusing, beautiful journey — you’re not lost. You’re just on the fun part of the ride. 🎢
posts.summary: I’ve been talking about my journey to a lot of my peers and team members lately. And every time I do, I’m reminded — it’s been anything but a straight line. Citibank taught me discipline and the art of building trust with clients.
